mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity cash flows are repriced higher.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are positive in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS change and returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, elevated borrowing costs constrain corporate investment and equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.4% over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals collapsing consumer demand and labor market stress, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through an earnings recession channel.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE does not exhibit a Spearman rank correlation more negative than -0.05 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to predict downside in more than 50% of joint adverse episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals imminent earnings compression as capacity utilization falls, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during late-cycle expansion regimes over any 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in more than 55% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in quarterly real GDP (GDPC1), measured by vintage changes, prompts an immediate upward repricing of macroeconomic fundamentals and corporate cash flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive vintage GDPC1 surprises are not positive in more than 55% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window, or the rank information coefficient falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real earnings expectations and increases the probability of Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of rising CPIAUCSL are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of negative return episodes falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a three-month window signals acute labor market deterioration that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days via collapsing consumption forecasts.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger is activated over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) forecast reduced construction activity, mortgage credit growth, and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the housing transmission channel weakens.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST declines are not negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 45%.
